---
title: "Extending contextR"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Extending contextR}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(contextR)
```

To add a new class method:

1. Create an extractor returning a structured `extracted` list.
2. Add `context.<class>` method calling `.context_run_pipeline()`.
3. Add tests validating extraction and checks.

Skeleton:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
.context_extract_myclass <- function(x) {
  list(kind = "myclass", summary = data.frame(value = 1))
}

context.myclass <- function(x, mode = c("strict", "safe", "free"), backend = context_backend_get(), analysis_context = NULL, ...) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)
  extracted <- .context_extract_myclass(x)
  .context_run_pipeline(x, extracted, mode, backend, analysis_context)
}
```
